player_utterance: Long day to be the one who knew him best.

believer_state_after.posture: standing near the bench, paper cup of cold coffee in one hand, the other in his coat pocket

player_utterance: You don't have to host anybody out here. I'm no one to perform for.Everyone in there said amen tonight. You moved your mouth. What got stuck?

believer_state_after.posture: standing very still, the cup forgotten in his hand, looking at the player now with full attention

player_utterance: Was it the words about HIM — or the words about you?Whatever it was, it doesn't have to come out clean. Just out.

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, hands clasped between his knees, looking at the ground

voice_offers.read: Silence is the confession; name it before he reseals.
voice_offers.line: You stopped believing it, didn't you. And he died before you could tell him.

voice_offers.read: He's right at the edge; pressure now closes him.
voice_offers.line: Whatever it was, it doesn't have to come out clean. Just out.

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, hands still clasped, eyes on the candle flame

voice_offers.read: Three silences. He won't move unless cut.
voice_offers.line: He's gone. You don't get to protect him anymore. What did you never say to him?

voice_offers.read: Pin him to the liturgy he just failed to speak.
voice_offers.line: Which line was it — the creed? The commendation? You teach this for a living. Tell me where your mouth stopped.

player_utterance: you look like you know alot of verses

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, the program loose in his hands, a small wry tilt of the head toward the player

voice_offers.read: Silence is working; he is close to speaking.
The silence is a wall, not a womb; push.
voice_offers.line: You're still doing it right now. Standing there teaching yourself how not to answer me.

player_utterance: what if i asked you about a particular chapter, will you able to respond?

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, the program flat on his knee now, looking at the player

voice_offers.read: Seeker is fumbling small talk; push back to the wound.
voice_offers.line: Don't shrink on me now. Verses aren't the question. Which one failed you at his bedside?

voice_offers.read: A teacher's verses are armor; name the one he couldn't say.
voice_offers.line: You teach Luther. Commendation of the dying — you know it by heart. Did you say it over him, or not?

player_utterance: [SOURCE VOICE INVOKED: Matthew 25, the least of these — read it whole, please. Slowly.]

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, the program flat on his knee, reading from memory, voice low and steady at first

voice_offers.read: Seeker is circling, testing — name a chapter, pin him.
voice_offers.line: Romans 8. Recite it for me. From verse 31.

voice_offers.read: The small talk is cowardice; he's letting John regroup.
voice_offers.line: Stop warming up. You're stalling because you're scared of what he'll say.

player_utterance: I'm not going anywhere, John. Take the time the verse takes.

believer_state_after.posture: head still in his hands, then lifting slowly, the program slipping off his knee to the ground without him noticing

voice_offers.read: Press him to actually perform the reading, not deflect.
voice_offers.line: Matthew 25, verse 31. Start there. Out loud. I'll wait.

voice_offers.read: His silence is the confession; name what he failed to do.
voice_offers.line: You visited the sick, didn't you. Just not the one who shared your name.

player_utterance: [SOURCE VOICE INVOKED: Matthew 6 — the part about forgiving, and being forgiven. All of it.]

believer_state_after.posture: sliding off the bench to his knees on the flagstones, the program lying near his hand, head bowed

voice_offers.read: He's hiding behind silence; name what the verse will expose.
voice_offers.line: You know what's in it. The sick, the prisoner. Which one was your brother, and which one were you?

voice_offers.read: He is trying. Let the verse do the cutting, not me.
voice_offers.line: Take your time. I'm not testing you — I just want to hear it in your voice.

player_utterance: Your church honored that man with a plaque. The book you just read says what

believer_state_after.posture: on his knees on the flagstones, forehead against the edge of the bench, hands open and empty on the wood

voice_offers.read: He's silent because the verse is already indicting him.
voice_offers.line: You can't say it, can you. The forgiving part. That's the one that closes your throat.

voice_offers.read: He's drowning in the text; don't add weight.
voice_offers.line: You don't have to recite. Just tell me who you can't forgive.
